techniques.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: PowerShell
techniques.evidence: The threat actors used PowerShell to download and execute a new Cobalt Strike PowerShell beacon in memory on the beachhead host.

techniques.name: Malicious File
techniques.evidence: The intrusion began with a password protected zipped ISO file that, when extracted and mounted, presented a single file named documents, which, when opened, started a hidden DLL file containing the Bumblebee malware loader.

techniques.name: OS Credential Dumping: LSASS Memory
techniques.evidence: The threat actor used Procdump to dump LSASS, and later used a built-in Windows tool, comsvcs.dll, to dump LSASS on the beachhead host.

techniques.name: Use of Kerberoasting
techniques.evidence: The threat actor executed the PowerShell module Invoke-Kerberoast.

techniques.name: Remote Services: Remote Desktop Protocol
techniques.evidence: The threat actor used RDP to access a server using the local Administrator account.

techniques.name: System Services: Service Execution
techniques.evidence: The threat actor used remote services to execute Procdump, which was used to dump LSASS.

techniques.name: Windows Management Instrumentation
techniques.evidence: The threat actor used Windows utilities like ping and tasklist for discovery tasks.

techniques.name: Remote System Discovery
techniques.evidence: The threat actor used Adfind for Active Directory discovery.

techniques.name: System Service Discovery
techniques.evidence: The threat actor used Windows utilities like ping and tasklist for discovery tasks.

techniques.name: Remote Access Tools
techniques.evidence: The threat actor deployed AnyDesk, which was the only observed persistence mechanism used during the intrusion.
